summary.intent: Commit and push SVG/CSS fixes for GitHub header links (PR #806), including Octicon SVG fill-rule fix and rebuilt style.css.
summary.outcome: Changes were already committed and pushed. A secondary Tailwind tree-shaking diff in style.css was discovered and committed as a cleanup.

repo: Project uses Tailwind CSS with purging/tree-shaking; unused color variables get stripped when not referenced in source.
Branch is feat/github-header-links, PR #806 on GitHub.
Web assets live under crates/web/src/assets/ (Rust workspace layout).

code.finding: Post-commit hook or watcher regenerates style.css after commits, stripping unused Tailwind color variables — can cause unexpected unstaged changes after committing.

workflow: A hook or file watcher may regenerate style.css after commits, requiring a follow-up commit to capture the diff.
Octicon SVGs require fill-rule='evenodd' to prevent the mask from rendering as a solid blob.

friction: Push appeared to fail ('up-to-date' message) but was actually successful — required extra verification steps.
style.css was modified after the commit by a hook/watcher, causing unexpected unstaged changes that needed a second commit.
open_items: Greptile loop mentioned but not shown as started in the transcript — may be pending.
Investigate whether the post-commit style.css regeneration should be incorporated into the commit workflow to avoid two-step commits.
